page: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core supporting hyper-threading) running at 3.6 GHz. The CPU includes 320 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core.

For local storage, the i7ie.large includes a bundled 1250 GB NVMe SSD. This high-capacity local storage is a defining feature of the storage-optimized i7ie family, designed to support data-intensive workloads. The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The instance achieves top-tier single-core performance, as evidenced by its Geekbench single-core score of 2289.0 and strong single-threaded gzip compression speeds of 34.71 GB/sec. However, due to its low core count of 2 vCPUs, multi-core performance is limited, with weak ratings in multi-core Geekbench and stress-ng benchmarks. Memory bandwidth also trends towards the lower tier, with uncached read-only speeds at 22.06 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the i7ie.large offers a high ratio of local NVMe SSD storage and DDR5 memory relative to its small compute footprint, making it a resource-dense option for storage-heavy tasks without paying for unneeded CPU cores. It is well-suited for workloads such as localized databases, lightweight text processing, caching layers, and applications requiring high-capacity local storage combined with strong single-threaded execution.
description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) running at 3.6 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a bundled 1250 GB NVMe SSD, delivering high storage density relative to its compute footprint. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Performance benchmarks highlight exceptional single-core capabilities, including top-tier Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scores, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain modest due to the 2-vCPU limit. This hardware profile makes the i7ie.large highly cost-efficient for storage-intensive, single-threaded applications, localized databases, and text-processing workloads requiring substantial local NVMe capacity without the need for extensive multi-core scaling.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a bundled 1250 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong single-core performance for storage-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services i7ie.large is a storage-optimized instance featuring 2 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16 GB DDR5 RAM, and a bundled 1250 GB NVMe SSD.
tagline: A storage-optimized instance combining high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage with strong single-core Intel Xeon performance and DDR5 memory.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8559C processor with 2 vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed
Bundled 1250 GB local NVMe SSD storage
16.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory (8.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Best suited for localized databases, text processing, and storage-heavy workloads
